texts.full_text: requiring high sensitivity to red and near infrared wavelengths. Additionally, the presence of multiple layers and elements can sometimes lead to signal degradation or reduced quantum efficiency due to increased parasitic absorption and reflection losses. The present invention aims to overcome these limitations by providing a novel structure and fabrication method that enhances the light-absorbing capability of back-illuminated solid-state imaging devices, particularly for long wavelengths. This is achieved through innovative design features such as optimized layer thicknesses, advanced interlayer materials with tailored optical properties, and efficient charge separation and transport mechanisms within the device architecture. By improving the absorption efficiency across a broad spectral range, including red and near infrared wavelengths, the back-illuminated solid-state imaging device according to the present invention is particularly well-suited for high-resolution imaging applications in fields such as photography, medical diagnostics, surveillance, and remote sensing. The enhanced performance of the imaging device can also lead to more compact and energy-efficient imaging systems, benefiting various industries including consumer electronics, automotive, and aerospace. texts.full_text: des students with a holistic approach to learning. Today The Marin School continues to thrive in San Rafael. It has evolved into a high-quality college-preparatory institution, committed to nurturing and challenging its diverse student body. The school offers advanced placement courses, small class sizes for personalized attention, and an environment that promotes individual growth through academic excellence and community involvement. In addition to rigorous academics, the Marin School emphasizes extracurricular activities such as sports, arts programs, and clubs, fostering a well-rounded educational experience for its students. The school's faculty is dedicated to providing mentorship and guidance to each student, preparing them not only academically but also personally. The institution has established partnerships with local businesses and organizations, allowing students opportunities for internships, volunteer work, and hands-on learning experiences that complement their classroom education. These collaborations enhance the curriculum by bringing real-world knowledge into the educational setting. Future Looking towards the future, The Marin School aspires to expand its reach in providing exceptional educational services. It plans on incorporating advanced technology in classrooms, expanding its arts programs, and further integrating sustainable practices into school life. With a commitment to continual improvement and innovation, TMS seeks to develop well-rounded individuals who are equipped with the necessary skills for success beyond high school. As of 2023, The Marin School stands as an exemplary educational institution that offers a unique blend of traditional education combined with progressive teaching methods designed to meet the diverse needs of its student body. It continues to serve as a beacon in the community by offering a nurturing environment where students can thrive academically and personally. Note:

texts.full_text: update their professional skills in a particular area. The modules are taught over one term, usually for one morning or one afternoon per week with 30 taught hours of lectures and practicals, followed by a coursework assessment or exam (which is optional for short course students). If the assessment is completed successfully, the module credit can count towards a future Master's degree. Many of these modules are CILIP accredited and so suitable for CPD for Library and Information professionals. DH modules currently offered as Short Courses include: Digital Resources in the HumanitiesElectronic PublishingInternet TechnologiesIntroduction to Programming Legal and Social Aspects of Electronic PublishingServer Programming and Structured DataXML Acceptance onto the module is by agreement by the tutor. Many other modules from taught programmes offered by UCL Department of Information Studies (DIS) are available as short courses. Details about fees and how to apply are also available on that page. MA/MSc in DigitalHumanities The MA/MSc programme, offered via UCL Department of Information Studies, is a new interdisciplinary degree exploring the intersection of digital technologies and culture. Short Courses in DH It is also possible to study individual modules from the MA/MSc programme as Short Courses such as: XML Internet Technologies Digital Resources in the Humanities These courses can contribute to professional development, and count towards the MA/MSc, should it be pursued further. Doctorates We have a variety of PhD students who are undertaking research with us, and are interested in hearing from those who are considering undertaking PhD research in Digital Humanities. texts.full_text: this includes providing them with clear understanding of their importance during these turbulent times. This is not an attempt at a simple categorization or dismissal. Rather it's acknowledging that those who continue to work are doing so in the most essential roles—often risking personal health as well comforts for our collective wellbeing amidst this crisis. As much support and affirmation we can extend towards these front-line workers, ensuring their welfare must be paramount during such trying times. On a deeper level of understanding it's clear that while others may find themselves in less visible roles or even not working at all due to the pandemic’s impact on economies around our world; there is an innate need for compassion and recognition towards those who are both struggling with their job security as well resolutely persevering. This divide, though starkly emphasized during this global health crisis, carries valuable lessons. It demonstrates how diverse but deeply interconnected we all truly live to be in a single community—whether it's our local communities or across the world. As each of us manages through these unique circumstances set by COVID-19; there are opportunities for every individual’s contribution and commitment can mean so much. Therefore, as citizens within this divided nation but also partake actively towards its unity: let love that unites stand firm while acknowledging those at different stations in life with respect. This is the essence of our shared humanity—the empathy we extend to one another transcends roles or societal expectations. In a world riven by such complexities, it's within this simplicity and kindness where true solidarity begins. Multi-Factor Survival Modeling ----------------------------------- We found multiple variables to have an independent association with survival. Moreover, single variable risk stratification is of limited use for its restrictive nature. For instance, it may so turn out that a fraction of first line patients relapse soon after the graft. If we create a rather simplistic single factor staging scheme just based on the relapse (after remission) status, it may under-predict for those at risk. For multi-factor modeling exercise, a major hindrance is small sample size. Commonly used methods such as survival tree ([@B39]) requires a large number of samples to produce a meaningful model. For instance, the International Staging System (ISS) was built on clinical and laboratory data of about 10,000 patients ([@B26]). We first examined the heterogeneity in the patient population using spectral clustering (Methods). All 39 pre-transplant variables were used for this. We obtained two clusters that showed a stark difference in survival pattern both for PFS (*P* \< 0.001) and OS (*P* \< 0.001). See [Figures 1](#F1){ref-type="fig"}, [2](#F2){ref-type="fig"} for the associated Kaplan Meier analysis. We marked the patient-groups mirrored by the clusters as high-risk and low-risk depending on their survival trend. texts.full_text: rd (Web) What's an "Account"? Rafael H. January 21, 2020 19:43 Updated An account is where we keep our money. When we talk about accounts, the first thing that comes in mind is the bank accounts - because if I keep my money in a bank like Bank of America, for example, I have an account at Bank of America. But... what if I keep my money under the mattress? When it comes to our financial control, the mattress can also be an account! When you sign up for Mobills, there's already a registered account called "Wallet". The "wallet" account can be the money that you keep with you and "walks" with you, anywhere you are - in your pocket, backpack or in a wallet, for example. Other common examples of accounts include credit cards (both debit and charge), investment portfolios like stocks or mutual funds, loans such as mortgages or personal loans that need to be repaid over time. In Mobills specifically: - Bank Accounts: You can add your various bank account details so you keep track of all the transactions occurring in each one. This helps maintain a holistic view of where and how much money is flowing. - Credit/Debit Cards: Adding these allows for tracking spending directly linked to purchases made via cards, helping manage budgets effectively. - Investments Accounts: For those who have investments like stocks or mutual funds, adding them as accounts ensures that changes in investment values are tracked over time. In essence, any place where you store your money can be considered an "account" for the purpose of financial tracking and management with Mobills. texts.full_text: issues with current anti-drug policies, emphasizing their overall inefficacy. The primary issue is the seemingly endless cycle of supply and demand; despite efforts to eliminate trafficking organizations or specific sources through law enforcement actions ("vast expenditures"), new entities quickly fill any voids created in this illicit market due its high profitability nature - thus rendering these victories short lived at best when considering both sides comprehensive view over time horizon for production/consumption statistics worldwide which remain largely unchanged even after significant effort has been put forth by authorities globally. Secondly, the human cost and financial implications are starkly negative; thousands lose their lives either directly or indirectly because of related violence caused within this underworld economy while taxpayers bear an extensive economic burden funding these unsuccessful initiatives aiming at curbing drug-related crimes with minimal tangible results so far presented through official reports highlighting its shortcomings extensively year after years without success stories coming forward during that period. To address the problems outlined above, a fundamental shift in approach might be necessary: 1 - Focus on Harm Reduction rather than eradication by providing safe consumption spaces and extensive education about substance abuse prevention while offering accessible treatment options for those struggling with addiction issues instead criminalizing personal usage which could potentially discourage people seeking help due its stigmatization. 2., Legalize certain substances or regulate their production/distribution tightly so as not only reduce associated crime rates but also generate tax revenue much like successful models adopted across some countries already shown effective outcomes such Canada’s legalization

Karen Frank is a woman on a mission – to change the way the world does networking and marketing forever. texts.full_text: PEKA - Washburn University’s joy over one of its own going to the pros quickly shifted to grief this weekend after a shooting killed a current Washburn football player and wounded the former member of the team who was drafted by the New York Giants just hours earlier. Police in Topeka, Kansas, where the university is located, said officers found 23-year-old defensive back Dwane Simmons dead when they investigated gunshots shortly before 1 a.m. Sunday. Cornerback Corey Ballentine was wounded, but his injuries were not believed to be life-threatening. Ballentine, also 23, grew up in Topeka and was picked by the Giants on Saturday in the sixth round of the NFL draft. AThe shooting has sent shockwaves through both Washburn University’s community and professional football circles as news spread about one player losing his life while another narrowly escaped serious injury. Simmons played for several seasons at Division II school, earning all-conference honors before graduating in May with a degree from the Lee Kuney School of Business. Depression as a risk factor for mortality has been shown to be comparable in strength to smoking \[[@B18]\]. The Global Burden of Disease (GBD) study quantifies the health effects of more than 100 diseases and injuries and found that in 2004, unipolar depression was the leading cause of disability in middle- and high-income countries \[[@B19]\]. Participants with positive screens for depression could potentially be referred for a more comprehensive evaluation by a professional qualified in diagnosing and managing depression. Guidelines provided by the American Heart Association (AHA) \[[@B20]\], the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force (USPSTF) \[[@B21]\], and the National Institute for Health and Clinical Excellence (NICE) \[[@B22]\] all recommend this protocol. texts.full_text: e Football League for Coventry City, Hereford United, Birmingham City and Wolverhampton Wanderers. After ten years as a professional at Coventry City he joined Birmingham City for a fee of £10,000 raised through a supporters' "Buy a Player Fund". He won the club's Player of the Year award for the 1981–82 season, marking a high point in his career with Birmingham City. Known for his versatility, he could play as a centre-back or full-back, often forming part of a resilient defensive unit. During his six seasons at Birmingham (1976–1982), Roberts became a fan favorite, notching over 150 league appearances and contributing to the team's rise in the Second Division (now EFL Championship). After leaving Birmingham, he joined Wolverhampton Wanderers in 1982, where he continued to be a consistent performer, earning a reputation for his tenacity and leadership. Roberts remained with Wolves until 1983 before moving to Hereford United, a club rooted in the Football League’s fourth tier at the time. His career total of over 400 league appearances reflects his durability and adaptability. A noted captain and role model, Roberts also earned two England B caps, a testament to his potential on the national stage. In retirement, he remained connected to the game as a coach and non-playing director at Coventry City, guiding youth development programs. His legacy at Birmingham City is particularly celebrated; a special benefit match was organized in 1986 to honor the fans’ initial “Buy a Player Fund” initiative, which secured his transfer. Roberts’ story became iconic for the club’s supporters, embodying community engagement and grassroots enthusiasm in football. He remains a cherished figure

texts.full_text: uch as: Debug.Assert(Dispatcher.CheckAccess()); // don't do marshaling here- it's already runinng on UI thread! Does anybody know what happens if I remove this code? Or, able answer my questions? A: That's a debugging assertion. Removing it willmake your program silently continue without the expected behavior when running in debug mode. The `Dispatcher.CheckAccess()` method is used to check whether you are currently executing on an appropriate thread (in this case, usually a UI-thread). When working with Windows Forms or WPF applications that interact directly through user interface elements like buttons and textboxes from other threads than they were created upon - it's critical not only for debugging but also ensuring your code doesn't have race conditions. If you remove the `Debug.Assert(Dispatcher.CheckAccess())`, then: - The program will still continue executing. - However, if this line is false (i.e., running on a non-GUI thread), any operation trying to interact with GUI elements directly can lead towards exceptions such as InvokeRequired or Cross-thread operations not permitted. This could potentially crash your application during runtime. In general: 1) Always check for the UI access from threads that are interacting using controls. 2）When removing checks, make sure you understand why and have a clear strategy on how to handle potential race conditions appropriately if they occur in production environment (for example by adding necessary marshalling). 3)(Note: The above points apply especially when working with WPF or WinForms applications). For newer UI frameworks like Blazor/ASP.NET Core, threading models may differ. Remember that assert statements are a tool for debugging and testing - once your code is in production they don't serve much purpose except possibly cluttering the output. Use them to make sure things behave correctly when you're developing/testng; then either remove or ignore these checks before releasing it

texts.full_text: [Laboratory control in diagnosis and treatment of infective endocarditis]. Infectious endocarditis is caused by a wide variety of microorganisms. Etiological diagnosis depends either on direct methods, directed to the detection and isolation of the causative organisms, or serological methods. Blood cultures remain as the best method for the diagnosis of bacterial endocarditis. Blood culture technology has changed due to the appearance of new culture techniques and automated systems, which have improved the sensitivity and specificity in detecting the causative agents. In addition to blood cultures, other methods such molecular biology techniques (e.g., PCR) and serological tests can be employed for a more rapid and accurate diagnosis. The choice of antimicrobial therapy is based on the results of susceptibility testing and the specific pathogen involved. Initial antibiotic therapy may be guided by the most common causative organisms in infective endocarditis, which include Staphylococcus aureus, Streptococcus species, and Gram-negative bacilli. Once the etiological agent is identified, antibiotics should be tailored accordingly. In addition to appropriate antimicrobial therapy, surgical intervention may be necessary in cases

CONCLUSION: Laparoscopic WR remains a feasible and minimally invasive method for gastric lesions≤5 cm regardless of location, with the favorable short term outcomes. texts.full_text: g left England. Going on into the 1980s: - Continued work at many industrial roles including livestock farming. - Deepened my passion for nature, animals, and outdoor pursuits which became a big component of our family life. The early '70's to mid ‘80’s had me expanding beyond agriculture by becoming involved in big business ventures that allowed flexibility while ensuring I would be there when the kids needed help. 1985-2003: During this time, my parents grew up and started their own adventures; they both had an interest similar to mine - a passion for travel and adventure. This was probably around time where technology was playing bigger role in our lives. I stopped working but had part-time roles that allowed me more independence. Later years into 2013: Retired from full-time work, now having the peace of retirement after several years spent nurturing both family life alongside career development and growth. My gypsy soul remains alive as I continue to travel within New England when possible while also focusing on a life filled with adventure. My life begins today - discovering new experiences but remembering every memory made along this winding trail called Life. Life is perhaps the best of all adventures, full of twists & turns that shape who we are. texts.full_text: Amphiphysin 1 (APLN). The BIN domain in both these adaptor molecules plays a crucial role by mediating interactions between cytoskeletal elements, actin filaments or microtubules \[[@CR9]--[@CR12]\]. Unlike APLN which is primarily found at the synaptic vesicle recycling stations within neurons and muscle cells to promote endocytosis of these structures upon neurotransmitter release events in nerve terminals for subsequent reuse, BIN1 appears more broadly involved with membrane dynamics across various cellular contexts \[[@CR13]--[@CR16]\]. This broader tissue distribution suggests a potential role that extends beyond the synaptic vesicle recycling function attributed to APLN. Unlike its close paralog Amphiphysin 1, BIN1 is broadly expressed in several tissues, with the highest expression in skeletal muscle \[[@CR2], [@CR4], [@CR6], [@CR11]\].
